title: Explain condition variables in Java. How are they used?

answers.junior: Condition variables let threads wait for certain conditions before continuing.
answers.middle: Condition variables are used with locks to signal threads. Use await(), signal(), and signalAll() methods.
answers.senior: Condition variables enable fine-grained thread coordination. Use with ReentrantLock for advanced signaling. Prefer over wait/notify for complex scenarios.
answers.tricky: Use condition variables for producer-consumer and complex coordination. Analyze signaling, spurious wakeups, and lock scope. Prefer java.util.concurrent for robust design.
